---
title: "FAQ --- Common Workshop Issues"
---

The questions that come up every workshop. If yours isn't here, ask on the workshop's chat channel (see [About](about.qmd)) and we'll add it.

## Setup & installs

### `BiocManager::install()` is taking forever / hanging on a single package

The most common cause is a slow Bioconductor mirror. Pick a closer one:

``` r
chooseBioCmirror()        # interactive; pick a US mirror if you're in the US
options(BioC_mirror = "https://bioconductor.org")
```

Then re-run `BiocManager::install()`.

### Mac: "compilation failed" / clang errors during install

You're missing the Xcode command-line tools. In a terminal:

``` bash
xcode-select --install
```

Re-launch RStudio after it finishes. If you also see `gfortran not found`, install [gfortran for macOS](https://mac.r-project.org/tools/) and retry.

### Windows / WSL: R can't see the project files

Don't keep your project on the Windows side (`/mnt/c/Users/...`) and run R from WSL --- file I/O is dramatically slower and case-sensitivity differs. Either:

-   Run R + RStudio **inside** WSL (recommended for advanced users), with the project under `~/`, or
-   Run **RStudio for Windows** with the project on the Windows side.

Pick one and stick with it.

### "RStudio can't find my newly installed packages"

Almost always a stale R session. Restart R: *Session → Restart R* (or `Ctrl/Cmd + Shift + F10`). If a package was installed from a different R version, run:

``` r
.libPaths()                       # which library is being used?
installed.packages()[, "Package"] # what's actually installed there?
```

If the version doesn't match, point RStudio at the correct R install: *Tools → Global Options → General → R version*.

### `Seurat` install fails with a Matrix / SeuratObject version mismatch

Reinstall the trio together, in this order:

``` r
install.packages(c("Matrix", "SeuratObject", "Seurat"))
```

Then restart R.

## Datasets

### `Read10X()` says "Barcode file missing" or "matrix file missing"

You almost certainly pointed at the wrong directory. The argument should be the **folder** containing `barcodes.tsv.gz`, `features.tsv.gz`, and `matrix.mtx.gz`, **not** any of those files. Run:

``` r
list.files("data/filtered_feature_bc_matrix")
```

You should see exactly those three filenames.

### Tutorial says `../data/...` but my data is in `data/`

The tutorials assume each `.qmd` lives one level deep (e.g. `tutorials/Tutorial_01.qmd`) so `../data/` resolves to a sibling `data/`. If you're running from the repo root, change the paths to `data/...` --- or move the tutorial into a subfolder.

### Download from 10x stalls or errors out

10x Genomics requires a click-through accept on each public dataset. Open the landing page in a browser, click *Download*, accept terms, and use the resulting URL. See [Datasets](Datasets.qmd).

## Running the analysis

### My UMAP doesn't match the slide / handout exactly

Expected. UMAP and clustering both depend on a random seed. The cluster identities and overall topology should match; rotation/reflection of the embedding will not. To reproduce exactly, set:

``` r
set.seed(2026)
seu <- RunUMAP(seu, dims = 1:30, seed.use = 2026)
seu <- FindClusters(seu, random.seed = 2026)
```

### `FindMarkers` is slow

Install [`presto`](https://github.com/immunogenomics/presto) (it's in the install script). Seurat will detect it automatically and use a much faster Wilcoxon implementation.

### I get warnings about future / parallelization

Most are safe to ignore. To silence and pick a sensible default, add at the top of your script:

``` r
options(future.globals.maxSize = 8 * 1024^3)   # 8 GB
future::plan("multicore", workers = 4)         # or "multisession" on Windows
```

### "Cannot allocate vector of size XX Gb"

You've run out of RAM. Options, in increasing order of effort:

1.  Restart R and only load the objects you need.
2.  Subset cells (`subset(seu, downsample = 5000)` for an exploration session).
3.  Use sparse-matrix operations (`Seurat` does by default; check that you haven't accidentally turned the assay into a dense matrix).
4.  Move to Talapas: a `--mem=64G` interactive job is plenty for most tutorials.

## Talapas / HPC

### "Permission denied (publickey, password)"

You either typed the password wrong, or your account isn't active yet. Reset at [https://duckid.uoregon.edu/](https://duckid.uoregon.edu/) and verify you can log in to a UO web service first. Email RACS if it persists.

### Duo prompt never arrives

Make sure the Duo Mobile app is open and your phone has data/Wi-Fi. If pushes don't work, type `2` instead of `1` at the Duo prompt to get a phone-call code, or `3` for a passcode.

### `srun` says "Invalid account or account/partition combination"

You forgot `--account=<myPIRG>` or your `SLURM_ACCOUNT` env-var isn't set. See the [SLURM Basics tutorial](Exercise_Folder/Tutorial_SLURM_Basics.html) §2.

### My job is `PD` (pending) forever

Run `squeue -j <jobid> --start` --- the *REASON* column tells you why. Common ones:

-   `Resources` --- you asked for more than the cluster currently has free; let earlier jobs drain.
-   `Priority` --- normal queueing; you'll run when it's your turn.
-   `ReqNodeNotAvail, Reserved for maintenance` --- your wall time runs into a maintenance window. Shorten `--time` or add `--time-min`.

### `scratch/` looks empty after a few months

`/scratch/<PIRG>/` purges files not accessed in **90 days**. Move important outputs to `/projects/<PIRG>/` or to LSRS for long-term storage. RACS does not back up Talapas.

## Workshop logistics

### Where do I download the tutorials?

[Exercises](Exercises.qmd) page → *Download* column. Each link is a raw `.qmd` file you open in RStudio.

### Can I work in Python / `scanpy` instead of R / Seurat?

Yes --- the lectures are framework-agnostic, and most tutorials include a Python equivalent in a `panel-tabset` block. The graded tutorials assume Seurat by default, but if you submit a working `scanpy` notebook that produces equivalent output, that's fine.

### I don't have a Talapas account --- can I still attend?

Yes. Everything except the **SLURM Basics** tutorial runs on a laptop. Talapas is recommended for the integration / large-dataset stretch goals but isn't required.

### Are recordings available?

See [About](about.qmd) for the recording policy and link.
